common.name: Name
home.subtitle: API Gateway to integrate external APIs with dynamic routing, centralized auth and per-API-key variables/rate limit (multi-tenant).
API Gateway to integrate external APIs with dynamic routing, centralized auth and per-API-key variables/rate limit (multi-tenant).
API Gateway to integrate external APIs with dynamic routing, centralized auth and per-API-key variables/rate limit (multi-tenant).
home.cards.passThrough.title: Full pass-through
home.cards.passThrough.desc: The request body is never modified. Only URL, headers and query params can be transformed.
home.cards.variables.title: Variables engine
home.cards.variables.desc: Detects {VAR} in templates and resolves them per API Key.
home.cards.observability.title: Observability
home.cards.observability.desc: Full request logs (headers + raw body + status + latency).
home.docs.title: How to use OpenRouteX
home.docs.intro: OpenRouteX works as a gateway in front of your upstream services. You register services and routes in the portal, then call a single public endpoint and OpenRouteX forwards the request to the configured upstream, applying upstream auth, variables and logging.
home.docs.quickStart.title: Quick start
home.docs.quickStart.1: Login to the portal and create a Service (API) with a unique slug.
home.docs.quickStart.2: Create one or more Routes (paths) for the service and configure the upstream URL/template.
home.docs.quickStart.3: Create an API Key for each client and set variables/limits for that key.
home.docs.quickStart.4: Call the gateway using the API-KEY header; monitor results, logs and metrics in Dashboard.
home.docs.publicUrl.title: Public URL
home.docs.publicUrl.note: Example: /accounts/invoices. The gateway matches apiSlug + method + path. API-KEY header is required.
home.docs.features.title: Main features
home.docs.features.services.title: Services:
home.docs.features.services.desc: group routes under a unique slug and set service-level options (like mTLS certificate).
home.docs.features.routes.title: Routes:
home.docs.features.routes.desc: map method+path to an upstream URL/template, optionally adding headers/query, forwarding client query, setting timeouts and applying upstream auth.
home.docs.features.apiKeys.title: API Keys:
home.docs.features.apiKeys.desc: authenticate clients via API-KEY header; define allowed services, variables per tenant and requests/min.
home.docs.features.variables.title: Variables:
home.docs.features.variables.desc: use {VAR} placeholders in target URL, Add Headers and Add Query. They resolve only from API Key variables (missing var returns 400).
home.docs.features.certificates.title: Certificates:
home.docs.features.certificates.desc: attach certificates (mTLS) to call HTTPS upstreams securely per service.
home.docs.features.logs.title: Logs & metrics:
home.docs.features.logs.desc: inspect request/response headers/body, final URL and latency; filter by slug/path/status/date.
home.docs.features.users.title: Users & permissions:
home.docs.features.users.desc: manage portal users, password reset emails and access permissions per menu tab.

users.permissions.primaryAdminFixed: Primary admin always has full access and permissions cannot be changed.
users.permissions.help: Select which menu tabs this user can access. To apply in the browser, logout/login.
users.permissions.createHelp: Select what this user can see and access in the menu.
users.permissions.noneWarning: Warning: with no permissions selected, the user won't be able to access any tab.

users.resetPasswordConfirm.body: This action will send a password reset link to {email}.
users.welcomeEmail.notSent.title: Email not sent
users.create.sendWelcomeEmail.help: Warn the user that an account was created and include a password reset link.
settings.timezone.label: Timezone
settings.timezone.help: Used to display dates/times in the portal.
settings.logsRetention.label: Logs retention
settings.logsRetention.help: Maximum time logs are kept in the database.
settings.logsRetention.success.help: Used to delete logs with HTTP 2xx.
settings.logsRetention.error.help: Used to delete logs with HTTP errors or missing status.
settings.logsCleanupInterval.label: Cleanup interval (minutes)
settings.logsCleanupInterval.help: How often the system runs the cleanup task.
settings.dashboard.metricsRefetch.help: How often the dashboard refreshes the charts.
settings.dashboard.logsRefetch.help: How often the dashboard refreshes the log list.
settings.dashboard.colorize.help: Enable/disable colored numbers (success/error/latency) in the endpoint report.
settings.gateway.title: Gateway
settings.gateway.description: Defaults applied by the proxy when a route does not override them.
settings.gateway.proxyTimeoutMs.help: If the route timeout is empty, this value is used.
settings.gateway.defaultForwardQuery.hel: Default value used when creating new routes.
settings.gateway.apiKeyHeaderName.help: Header used by the gateway to read the client API key.
settings.gateway.blockedHeaders.help: One per line. These headers are removed before calling upstream.
settings.security.title: Security
settings.security.description: Password policy and login lockout parameters.
If enabled and SMTP is configured, the user receives an alert.
settings.smtp.title: Email (SMTP)
settings.smtp.description: Used to send alerts (e.g., login lock).
settings.smtp.secure.help: false = STARTTLS (upgrade after connect). true = SMTPS (TLS from the start). Must match the chosen port.
settings.smtp.tlsRejectUnauthorized.help: Keep true in production. Set false only for self-signed certificates (not recommended).
settings.smtp.password.help: filling this field replaces the password.
settings.smtp.testTo.help: If empty, the system tries to send to the admin email.
